items.title: Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe Commerce is affected by an Incorrect Authorization vulnerability that could result in a Security feature bypass. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized read and write access.

items.title: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This vulnerability allows an unrestricted upload of a file with a dangerous type, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution in the context of the current user. Exploitation requires user interaction, such as visiting a crafted URL.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This was addressed in the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce | APSB26-73
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Cross-site scripting (XSS) in Adobe Commerce Form Field
items.summary: A cross-site scripting vulnerability was found in Adobe Commerce, specifically in a form field. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, leading to remote code execution via a crafted cookie. All versions before 1.11.12 are vulnerable.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered 'PolyShell', a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released APSB26-05, a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, and unauthorized code execution.
